title: Beach Party — Common Verbs
description: Ten verbs you'll use every day — EAT, DRINK, SLEEP, WALK, READ, WATCH, SWIM, DANCE, LAUGH, RUN — through a Saturday at the beach.
summary_pt_br: Dez verbos fundamentais do cotidiano em inglês, treinados num cenário de festa na praia. Presente simples com he/she/it -s revisitado.

scenes.text_en:
Saturday, 4 p.m. The beach is loud.
Rafa COOKS. Maya DANCES. Joca READS his laptop (he does that everywhere).
Cosmo RUNS after a ball. Mr. Kumar WATCHES, calm.
Dona Lúcia LAUGHS at a joke only she gets.
The sun SETS. The party RISES.
scenes.text_pt:
Sábado, 16h. A praia está barulhenta.
Rafa COZINHA. Maya DANÇA. Joca LÊ o laptop (faz isso em todo lugar).
Cosmo CORRE atrás de uma bola. Mr. Kumar ASSISTE, calmo.
Dona Lúcia RI de uma piada que só ela entende.
O sol SE PÕE. A festa COMEÇA.

scenes.title: Third-person -s (again, but real this time)
scenes.body_en: With he/she/it, add -s to the verb. Most verbs just add -s: eat → eats, sleep → sleeps. Verbs ending in -y after a consonant change -y to -ies: cry → cries, study → studies. Verbs ending in -s, -sh, -ch, -x, -o add -es: watch → watches, do → does, go → goes.
scenes.body_pt: Com he/she/it, adiciona -s. Maioria dos verbos: só -s (eat → eats). Verbos em -y depois de consoante: -y vira -ies (cry → cries). Verbos em -s, -sh, -ch, -x, -o: -es (watch → watches, go → goes).

exercise.question: Which is correct?
exercise.explanation: STUDY → STUDIES. Y after a consonant becomes IES.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Y depois de consoante: vira IES. Study → studies.

turns.en: Cosmo runs, swims, and eats — in that order.
turns.pt: O Cosmo corre, nada e come — nessa ordem.

turns.en: He sleeps like he runs. With his whole body.
turns.pt: Ele dorme igual corre. Com o corpo todo.

exercise.explanation: Five third-person patterns. Almost every verb fits one of these.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Cinco padrões. Quase todo verbo cabe em um.
